name: Italian Fig Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Mix flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder in a large bowl. Cut in butter until the mixture forms coarse crumbs. Make a well in the center and add eggs and vanilla extract, mixing with fingers or a fork until the dough leaves the sides of the bowl. Knead the dough until smooth, then set aside.
Immerse dried figs in hot water for about 5 minutes, drain, remove stems, and chop. Combine chopped figs and walnuts. Add cinnamon, brown sugar, and water. Cook over low heat until the fruit is soft and the filling has thickened. Set aside to cool.
Roll the dough out thinly on a floured surface. Cut into circles using a cookie cutter. Place 1 tablespoon of filling in the center, fold edges, and seal by moistening the edges with a little water. Bake at 350°F for 15-20 minutes or until golden brown.
